entries.label: Giebeler v. M&B Associates
entries.excerptText: Imposition of burdensome policies can interfere with disabled persons' right to use and enjoyment of their dwellings.
entries.proposition: Burdensome housing policies may require accommodation when they interfere with use and enjoyment.

entries.label: United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co.
entries.excerptText: The FHAA imposes an affirmative duty upon landlords reasonably to accommodate the needs of handicapped persons.
entries.proposition: Reasonable accommodation may require landlords to shoulder reasonable financial burdens.

entries.label: McGary v. City of Portland
entries.excerptText: The lien the City put on McGary's house prevents the full use and enjoyment of his property.
entries.proposition: Housing-related financial burdens can impair the full use and enjoyment of a dwelling.

entries.label: HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Accommodations
entries.citation: Joint Statement of the Department of Housing and Urban Development and the Department of Justice, Reasonable Accommodations under the Fair Housing Act (May 2004)
entries.excerptText: Reasonable accommodations may be necessary to afford a person with a disability equal opportunity to use and enjoy a dwelling.
entries.proposition: Equal use and enjoyment may require necessary accommodation changes.

entries.label: 24 CFR 982.316
entries.excerptText: The PHA must approve a live-in aide if needed as a reasonable accommodation.
entries.proposition: A PHA must approve a live-in aide when needed as a reasonable accommodation.
